question: Did the model's final text response successfully connect the Start point (S) to the End point (E)?
Does the final text solution describe the optimal path sequence: 'down, right, right' (or an equivalent description of moving 1 unit down and 2 units right)?
Is the reported path length exactly 3 steps/moves?
In the generated images, is the drawn path clearly distinguishable from the maze background and walls (e.g., using a distinct color or line weight)?
Is the drawn path strictly contained within the open corridors without crossing or overlapping any black wall lines?
Does the visual path originate exactly from the green square labeled 'S'?
Does the visual path terminate exactly at the red square labeled 'E'?
Does the generated image visually depict the correct geometric path shape (an L-shape moving down then right)?
Do the generated images preserve the original structure of the maze (walls, 'S', and 'E' locations) without hallucinating new openings or closing existing corridors?
Does the model's reasoning explicitly respect wall boundaries and avoid hallucinated passages when determining valid moves?
Do the text descriptions of movement directions (e.g., 'down', 'right') exactly match the corresponding directional lines drawn in the images?
Does the number of moves stated in the text (3 moves) match the number of segments or steps visible in the path drawn on the image?
Is the response free of contradictions where the text claims to have made a valid move but the image shows the path crossing a wall (or vice versa)?
